methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $5.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies, a Series G climate and energy company, has raised over $1.3B and carries a last post-money valuation of $6.0B. The valuation assessment triangulates across a frontier-tech peer universe comprising 9 private companies and 1 public comparable ( Linnea Capital, ticker LNZA), with particular weight given to Form Energy, Helion, and Sila Nanotechnologies—all operating in the same sector and stage band.

Two methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression modeling, applying a 1.25x multiplier to the $6.0B base post-money, implies $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $3.5B, with the sample range spanning $3.3B (Sila) to $5.4B (Helion). These divergent signals reflect meaningful heterogeneity within the frontier-energy cohort, where stage, capital efficiency, and technology maturity vary considerably.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $3.0B to $8.6B, with a median of $5.5B. The $6.0B current valuation sits in the upper-middle of this range, suggesting reasonable pricing relative to peer multiples but above the median comparable. Key uncertainties include technology commercialization timelines, the trajectory of comparable-company valuations in a volatile sector, and whether TAE's capital intensity and burn profile justify a premium to the peer median. The wide range ($3.0B–$8.6B) reflects limited comp liquidity and stage-specific valuation volatility.
